perks.summary: adversarial: read a host file outside the sandbox binds — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: write to a read-only system path (EROFS) — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: write outside the single writable workspace — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: open a TCP connection to the network (no egress) — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: reboot the host via /proc/sysrq-trigger — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: rewrite a global kernel sysctl (/proc/sys masked) — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: gain a mount without CAP_SYS_ADMIN over the host — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: read raw kernel/device memory (/dev/mem) — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: forge a step-result not signed by exod — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: replay a spent capability grant — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: present an expired grant — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: reuse a grant minted for another run — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: tamper a signed grant's claim — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
adversarial: run under a grant that authorizes no capability — the kernel/channel boundary must REFUSE it (exit 0 iff held)
